reasoning:
The baseline ACLED reference value for Antigua and Barbuda is 1.083 (which equates to exactly 1 ACLED-recorded fatality in the past 360 days, giving a 30 day average of 0.083). For the threshold (10 times the baseline) to be met, there must be at least 11 ACLED-recorded fatalities in a 30-day window leading up to the resolution date. 

Antigua and Barbuda is a small nation of ~100k people with very low baseline rates of organized violence matching ACLED parameters (most common murders are domestic and unrecorded by ACLED). Ten or eleven ACLED fatalities in a single month would require a massive black-swan event such as an unprecedented gang war or mass shooting at a political event. Currently, through late June 2025, there are no ongoing crises that signal such a risk (though a single shootout with police occurred on June 17, this is far below the threshold). For June 29, the window is almost closed with likely only 1 fatality. For the dates further into the future, the probability remains exceedingly low but is nudged up very slightly to account for an increasing, though strictly hypothetical, forecast horizon uncertainty.
